extraction.one_line_summary: MARTIN Dan attacked early on final climb into headwind, held off chasers including Thomas and Porte who couldn't bridge despite GC teams pulling.
tactical_patterns: Martin's solo attack succeeded into headwind specifically because first part of climb was raced so hard teammates were eliminated.
Richie Porte worked for Van Avermaet's yellow jersey defense rather than own GC, revealing BMC priority hierarchy early.
Quick-Step's early stage aggression forced BMC to conserve energy, allowing Van Avermaet to retain yellow despite expectations.
Thomas couldn't bridge to Martin despite superior GC strength, showing headwind negates normal power advantages on this finish type.

rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Attacked early into headwind and held off stronger GC riders, suggesting peak form after struggling entire season including being dropped at Flèche Wallonne
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: neutral: Rode for Van Avermaet's yellow defense instead of own position, indicating BMC hasn't committed to his GC yet
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Showed clear strength attempting to bridge to Martin but couldn't close gap into headwind
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: Retained yellow despite expectations of losing it, benefiting from Quick-Step forcing others to work early
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: positive: Second place finish at three seconds shows climbing legs present for future mountain stages
rider_signals.ANDERSON Soren Kragh: negative: Lost time with 5km to go, caught behind traffic and team cars
